claude.output_text:
{"findings":[
  {"finding_id":"F1-dual-resume-state","severity":"high","category":"maintainability","title":"1.12a names two resume state stores, violating the plan's own 'no second state store' invariant","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":286,"symbol":"1.12a DoD","snippet":"Resume-critical state is mirrored to BEADS child issues / `.beads/context/execution-state.md`; checkpoint drift is detectable."},"recommendation":"Pick one source of truth for resume state. BEADS child issues already qualify. Delete the `.beads/context/execution-state.md` mirror, or explicitly demote it to a read-only derived render of BEADS (same status as the checkpoint file). Otherwise the Risks section's 'Do not add a second state store' line at 352-355 is contradicted by its own DoD.","confidence":0.9,"fingerprint":"swarm-112-dual-resume-state"},

  {"finding_id":"F2-dag-skips-readonly-stage","severity":"high","category":"bug","title":"1.12c jumps to parallel worktree execution, skipping 1.11's explicit 'read-only decomposition artifact first' step","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":316,"symbol":"1.12c DoD","snippet":"`decompose-plan` stage ... emit `depends_on` edges as first-class data ... Pipeline engine consumes the DAG: independent work units run in parallel worktrees via the existing Agent/Task infrastructure."},"recommendation":"Split 1.12c into two sub-phases: (a) promote decompose-plan to emit a machine-readable DAG artifact only (matches swarm-do/docs/plan.md:1397-1398 'first version creates a decomposition artifact, not parallel implementation branches'), gated on measurable reduction in 'task too broad' clarifications; (b) only then add the parallel executor. Do not collapse the gate.","confidence":0.9,"fingerprint":"swarm-112-dag-skips-readonly"},

  {"finding_id":"F3-knowledge-jsonl-shadow-source","severity":"high","category":"maintainability","title":"knowledge.jsonl is primed by agents before work, which makes telemetry a second source of truth for agent behavior","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":333,"symbol":"1.12d DoD","snippet":"Agents (research, writer, spec-review) prime from `knowledge.jsonl` through a small JSONL filter matching their file scope before starting"},"recommendation":"Gate knowledge priming behind a preset flag, ship the writer side first as an optional input channel (not default), and require priming output to be an overlay file logged to the run artifact rather than silently injected into role prompts. Explicitly list `knowledge.jsonl` in Risks/non-goals as a derived observability surface, not a behavior-steering store, or the 1.11 adoption filter #4 (swarm-do/docs/plan.md:1273-1274) no longer holds.","confidence":0.85,"fingerprint":"swarm-112-knowledge-shadow-source"},

  {"finding_id":"F4-watchdog-infeasible","severity":"high","category":"bug","title":"Writer token/tool-call budget watchdog has no stated mechanism; subagent tool calls are opaque to the parent","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":186,"symbol":"1.12-N1","snippet":"The watchdog counts observable tool-result events, elapsed wall time, and captured output bytes, and uses token estimates only when real token fields are unavailable. Defaults: `tool_calls=50`, `estimated_tokens=60_000`"},"recommendation":"Add an explicit open question / spike (parallel to open question #1 on PreCompact) to validate that orchestrator-side observation of subagent tool events is possible in headless Claude Code. Until that is proven, only the cooperative writer-side `HANDOFF_REQUESTED` path should be in 1.12a DoD; the watchdog should be a stretch goal. Numeric defaults (`tool_calls=50`, `estimated_tokens=60_000`) imply a live counter that the plan has no substrate for.","confidence":0.85,"fingerprint":"swarm-112-watchdog-infeasible"},

  {"finding_id":"F5-posttooluse-missing-run-context","severity":"high","category":"bug","title":"PostToolUse hook observation schema requires run_id/phase_id/bead_ids with no injection path","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":232,"symbol":"1.12-N3","snippet":"writes a structured observation row to `telemetry/observations.jsonl`. Schema: `ts`, `run_id`, `phase_id`, `event_type`, `tool`, `file_paths`, `bead_ids`, `diff_size_bytes`."},"recommendation":"Specify how the hook learns swarm-do run context. Options: (a) write a per-session `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA/active-run.json` that the dispatcher updates and the hook reads, (b) set env vars on Agent() invocation and require hooks to honor them, or (c) drop run-scoped fields from v1 and compute them post-hoc from a join on timestamps. Current spec is under-specified and will produce unjoinable observation rows.","confidence":0.85,"fingerprint":"swarm-112-posttooluse-missing-ctx"},

  {"finding_id":"F6-resume-vs-recover-naming","severity":"medium","category":"maintainability","title":"1.12 introduces `swarm resume <bd-id>` while 1.11 already names the same command `swarm recover`","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":249,"symbol":"1.12-N4","snippet":"Ship `swarm resume <bd-id>` and `/swarm-do:resume <bd-id>` as equivalent entry points."},"recommendation":"Reconcile with swarm-do/docs/plan.md:1423 ('future `swarm recover`'). Either rename this section's CLI to `swarm recover` for naming continuity, or amend Section 1.11 in the same change. Do not ship two commands for the same job.","confidence":0.95,"fingerprint":"swarm-112-resume-vs-recover"},

  {"finding_id":"F7-runs-v2-or-sidecar-fork","severity":"medium","category":"maintainability","title":"1.12a DoD leaves `runs.v2` vs companion `run_events.v1.jsonl` as an undecided either/or","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":294,"symbol":"1.12a DoD","snippet":"Telemetry: do **not** mutate frozen `runs.v1` in place. Ship either `runs.v2` or a companion `run_events.v1.jsonl` ... update reports/TUI to join it."},"recommendation":"Decide before 1.12a starts. The two paths have different telemetry joiner, retention ADR, and TUI work. Recommend sidecar `run_events.v1.jsonl` — it preserves the frozen v1 contract and matches the observations/knowledge JSONL pattern already proposed. Commit to one in the plan.","confidence":0.9,"fingerprint":"swarm-112-runs-v2-fork"},

  {"finding_id":"F8-checkpoint-path-unvalidated","severity":"medium","category":"bug","title":"Checkpoint artifact path `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/runs/<run_id>/checkpoint.v1.json` is asserted without aligning to existing run artifact layout","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":285,"symbol":"1.12a DoD","snippet":"`hooks/precompact.sh` writes a derived checkpoint artifact at `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/runs/<run_id>/checkpoint.v1.json` on `PreCompact` event."},"recommendation":"Cite the current swarm run artifact directory convention used by the Phase 9/10 telemetry (e.g. from `bin/swarm` and provider-findings storage in ADR 0003) and confirm this path matches. If it doesn't, adjust. `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA` is per-plugin, not per-run — pin the exact subdirectory scheme in the plan so it isn't decided in implementation.","confidence":0.7,"fingerprint":"swarm-112-checkpoint-path"},

  {"finding_id":"F9-precompact-blocking-open-question","severity":"medium","category":"bug","title":"1.12a DoD depends on PreCompact hook in headless Claude, which Open Question #1 admits is unproven","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":386,"symbol":"Open questions #1","snippet":"Does the PreCompact hook fire reliably in headless Claude Code (orchestrator subagent runs)? Needs a 10-minute spike before 1.12a"},"recommendation":"Promote this spike out of 'open questions' and make it a named prerequisite listed in the `Depends on:` header at line 5-6. If the hook does not fire in headless subagent runs, the checkpoint leg of 1.12a collapses onto the end-of-unit trap only — that is a different DoD.","confidence":0.85,"fingerprint":"swarm-112-precompact-gate"},

  {"finding_id":"F10-permissions-install-scope-ambiguous","severity":"medium","category":"security","title":"`swarm permissions install --role writer` target file scope is not specified","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":216,"symbol":"1.12-N2","snippet":"`swarm permissions install --role writer` performs a structural JSON parse/merge with an atomic write (`settings.local.json.tmp` + rename) on operator confirmation."},"recommendation":"Name the file explicitly (repo-local `.claude/settings.local.json` vs user `~/.claude/settings.local.json`) and default to repo-local. Require `--scope user` for global edits and print the absolute target path before the merge. Auto-modifying the wrong settings file is a hard-to-reverse blast radius.","confidence":0.85,"fingerprint":"swarm-112-permissions-scope"},

  {"finding_id":"F11-per-unit-merge-mechanism-missing","severity":"medium","category":"bug","title":"Per-work-unit 'merge to integration branch' contract assumes a branch strategy not defined elsewhere","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":323,"symbol":"1.12c DoD","snippet":"Per-work-unit commit: writer's DoD includes a commit on its worktree branch before spec-review runs. Orchestrator merges to integration branch after spec-review APPROVED."},"recommendation":"Cite or define the integration-branch flow (naming, rebase vs merge, conflict handling). Today writers run in worktrees but there is no formalized merge step in swarm-do/skills/swarm-do/SKILL.md. Without that, 'merges to integration branch' is a silent new orchestrator responsibility with its own failure modes.","confidence":0.8,"fingerprint":"swarm-112-integration-branch"},

  {"finding_id":"F12-orchestrator-runs-tests-boundary","severity":"medium","category":"maintainability","title":"1.12d has 'orchestrator runs pytest/ruff' without saying whether that lives in the Claude dispatcher or the Python engine","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":340,"symbol":"1.12d DoD","snippet":"Orchestrator runs `pytest` / `ruff` / preset-configured validator independently after each writer phase; failures are added to the spec-review input as objective evidence."},"recommendation":"Place this on one side of the SKILL.md engine boundary (swarm-do/skills/swarm-do/SKILL.md:41-50). Running shell validators is deterministic work — it belongs in a new `validate` pipeline stage run by the Python engine, surfaced to spec-review as a structured input. Do not ship it as an ad-hoc Bash call from the Claude dispatcher.","confidence":0.85,"fingerprint":"swarm-112-orchestrator-validates"},

  {"finding_id":"F13-scope-too-wide-for-dogfood","severity":"medium","category":"maintainability","title":"1.12a alone ships four net-new user-visible surfaces in a single sub-phase, which defeats chunked dogfood","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":283,"symbol":"1.12a","snippet":"1.12a — Context resilience ... `hooks/precompact.sh` ... `bin/swarm resume` ... Writer token/tool-call budget ... `observations.jsonl` schema + `PostToolUse` hook"},"recommendation":"Split 1.12a along the four independent landings: (i) PreCompact checkpoint + resume CLI, (ii) PostToolUse observations, (iii) writer budget watchdog/handoff, (iv) telemetry event stream decision. Each has its own promotion scorecard criterion already; there is no reason to merge them. The current 1.12a DoD cannot be safely dogfooded as one unit because a regression in any leg forces rollback of the others.","confidence":0.85,"fingerprint":"swarm-112-scope-wide"}
]}

codex.final_text: Invalid schema for response_format 'codex_output_schema': In context=('properties', 'findings', 'items'), 'additionalProperties' is required to be supplied and to be false.
